description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular wooden structure elevated on sturdy posts, featuring a prominent red gable roof with a secondary gable extension. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal wooden planks, giving it a rustic, utilitarian appearance. The front side includes a small, centered rectangular window beneath the roof peak, while the rest of the structure appears windowless. The open lower section, supported by four posts, provides ample space for livestock or storage. The roof is steeply pitched, constructed from red corrugated material, and extends well beyond the walls for added weather protection. The overall design is simple yet functional, typical of agricultural outbuildings found in rural or peri-urban settings.
description: A wooden cowshed with a red gable roof, elevated on posts, featuring a small front window and open lower section for livestock or storage.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main gable roof, a small centered window, and the open lower section supported by posts.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full expanse of the wooden wall and the overhanging red roof, with no windows or doors visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the broad, uninterrupted wooden wall and the full width of the red roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the wooden wall and roof overhang, with no openings.
attribute_door_style: none visible; likely open lower section for livestock access
attribute_window_presence: one small rectangular window on the front gable
